shelfmark: UTO Sinai Syriac NF frag 65, SPP No. 6

note: Script used by the Syrian Orthodox communities

work_wit.alt_title: De simplicium medicamentorum temperamentis ac facultatibus
note: Precise identification of this text remains to be done

cataloguer.message: Description and identification of undertexts as part of SPP (2011-2016). Migrated to SMDL in 2025.

program.label: Sinai Palimpsests Project
program.description: Imaged and studied as part of the Sinai Palimpsests Project (2011–2016), a collaboration between St. Catherine’s Monastery of the Sinai, the Early Manuscripts Electronic Library, and UCLA Library, funded by the Arcadia Fund and the Ahmanson Foundation. The project used multispectral imaging and scholarly analysis to recover and document erased and obscured texts in palimpsest manuscripts from the monastery’s collection.

bib.citation: Bhayro, Siam, Robert Hawley, Grigory Kessel, and Peter E. Pormann. "Collaborative research on the digital Syriac Galen palimpsest.” Semitica et Classica 5 (2012): 261-264. https://doi.org/10.1484/J.SEC.1.103064

Bhayro, Siam, Robert Hawley, Grigory Kessel, and Peter E. Pormann. "The Syriac Galen palimpsest: Progress, prospects and problems.” Journal of Semitic Studies 58 (2013): 131-148. https://doi.org/10.1093/jss/fgs042
Hawley, Robert. "More identifications of the Syriac Galen palimpsest.” Semitica et Classica 7 (2014): 237-272. https://doi.org/10.1484/J.SEC.5.103532
Brock, Sebastian P. Catalogue of Syriac Fragments (New Finds) in the Library of the Monastery of Saint Catherine, Mount Sinai. Mount Sinai Foundation, 1995
